req.title: System-Admin creates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path creation by a system-admin with all mandatory fields supplied (EP – valid partition); (2) submission with a mandatory field left blank, expecting a 400 validation error (NEG / DT – invalid input decision branch); (3) a boundary-value case where the task title is exactly at the maximum allowed length – 255 characters (BVA); (4) an unauthenticated attempt that must return 401 (NEG – error pathway); (5) a state-transition case that verifies the audit record is written only after a successful creation (STC – pre-creation state → post-creation state with audit). Together these five tests provide EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG coverage as required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4 and satisfy the ≥1 positive, ≥1 negative, ≥1 boundary constraint.

tests.name: test_admin_creates_onboarding_task_all_mandatory_fields
actions: POST /onboarding-tasks with all mandatory fields populated
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Returned task name matches submitted value
trace: R-WEB-025 – Expected outcomes: onboarding-task is created; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import admin_api_client, get_audit_record

def test_admin_creates_onboarding_task_all_mandatory_fields(admin_api_client):
    payload = {
        "name": "Test Onboarding Task EP",
        "description": "Standard onboarding checklist",
        "due_days": 30,
        "assigned_role": "new_hire"
    }
    resp = admin_api_client.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id"), "Entity id must be present in response"
    assert body["name"] == payload["name"]

tests.name: test_admin_create_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-025 – Error pathways: 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import admin_api_client

def test_admin_create_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(admin_api_client):
    payload = {
        "description": "Missing name field",
        "due_days": 30,
        "assigned_role": "new_hire"
    }
    resp = admin_api_client.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors") or body.get("detail") or ""
    assert "name" in str(errors).lower(), "Error must reference the missing 'name' field"

tests.name: test_admin_creates_onboarding_task_name_at_max_length
preconditions: Maximum allowed length for 'name' is 255 characters
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
trace: R-WEB-025 – Mandatory fields must be validated (boundary of field length constraint)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import admin_api_client

MAX_NAME_LEN = 255

def test_admin_creates_onboarding_task_name_at_max_length(admin_api_client):
    boundary_name = "B" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    payload = {
        "name": boundary_name,
        "description": "BVA max-length name",
        "due_days": 1,
        "assigned_role": "new_hire"
    }
    resp = admin_api_client.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id"), "Entity id must be present"
    assert len(body["name"]) == MAX_NAME_LEN, "Returned name length must equal 255"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_create_onboarding_task_returns_401
actions: POST /onboarding-tasks with valid payload but no auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import unauthenticated_client

def test_unauthenticated_create_onboarding_task_returns_401(unauthenticated_client):
    payload = {
        "name": "Unauth Task",
        "description": "Should be rejected",
        "due_days": 7,
        "assigned_role": "new_hire"
    }
    resp = unauthenticated_client.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got: {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_audit_record_written_after_successful_onboarding_task_creation
preconditions: Audit log does not yet contain an entry for the task to be created
actions: Record audit-log baseline count for resource type onboarding-task
Retrieve audit records filtered by the returned entity id
Assert a new audit entry exists referencing the created entity
expected: Exactly one audit record exists for the new entity id
Audit record action is 'CREATE' and actor is the system-admin user
trace: R-WEB-025 – Expected outcomes: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import admin_api_client, get_audit_records, admin_user_id

def test_audit_record_written_after_successful_onboarding_task_creation(
    admin_api_client
):
    payload = {
        "name": "Audit Verification Task",
        "description": "Verify audit trail",
        "due_days": 14,
        "assigned_role": "new_hire"
    }
    resp = admin_api_client.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    entity_id = resp.json()["id"]
    audit_entries = get_audit_records(entity_id=entity_id, resource="onboarding-task")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "At least one audit record must exist"
    latest = audit_entries[0]
    assert latest["action"].upper() == "CREATE"
    assert latest["actor_id"] == admin_user_id()
